method.rainfall_formula: Z = 300 × R^1.4; dBZ capped at 55 for rainfall volume
method.runoff_formula: NRCS direct runoff: S = 1000/CN − 10; Ia = 0.20S; Q = (P − Ia)²/(P + 0.80S) when P > Ia
method.runoff_coefficient_explanation: Event classification uses composite NRCS curve numbers from SSURGO hydrologic soil groups and 2021 NLCD land cover. Dry, normal, and wet antecedent-condition estimates expose uncertainty in initial loss.
method.peak_flow_formula: Screening peak CFS = 2 × runoff volume ÷ triangular hydrograph base time; base time = rain duration + 2 × NRCS watershed lag
method.peak_flow_explanation: Peak CFS is a routed screening estimate, not runoff volume divided by one hour. Lag uses USGS 3DEP terrain, supplied pour point, and basin extent.
method.target_formula: Fill target = 52,442 ft³ × (technical-section length ÷ 0.75 mi) × (1 + canyon pothole modifier)
method.target_explanation: Zero G is anchored to the 1-meter depression inventory of 114 depressions totaling 1,485.0 m³. Other targets are normalized by technical-section length and adjusted using user-assigned canyon morphology modifiers.
method.spatial_formula: 50+ dBZ over 50% of the watershed, or 55+ dBZ over 25%, or 60+ dBZ over 10%
method.spatial_explanation: The same watershed-percentage gate applies to every canyon. It prevents light basin-wide rain or one isolated radar pixel from being labeled full.
method.fill_ratio_explanation: Estimated fill ratio = normal-antecedent-condition direct runoff ÷ canyon storage target. A value of 1.0 means modeled runoff equals the provisional empty-storage target; it is not a measured pool-depth percentage.
method.atlas_explanation: Atlas context compares watershed-average radar accumulation with duration-interpolated NOAA Atlas 14 point-frequency depths at the canyon outlet.
method.scaling_basis: Technical-section length replaces watershed-area scaling. Pothole modifiers represent storage density and typical pool size relative to Zero G.

sources.label: IEM N0Q raster and dBZ encoding
NWS radar rainfall estimation and default Z–R relationship

classification.minor: Runoff ratio below 1.0 and no heavy-rain footprint gate; little to no pool-depth change expected
classification.moderate: Runoff ratio at least 1.0 or a heavy-rain footprint gate was reached, but the complete likely-full test was not met
classification.likely_full: Runoff ratio at least 1.0, spatial gate reached, and at least two wet frames
classification.full_flush: Runoff ratio at least 2.0, spatial gate reached, and at least two wet frames

limitations: Only Zero G has a mapped depression-volume anchor. Other targets remain provisional until field observations or canyon-specific terrain analysis calibrate technical length and pool-storage modifiers.
Radar reflectivity is an indirect rainfall estimate and values above 55 dBZ can be hail-contaminated.
Curve numbers and routing are screening estimates; antecedent moisture, bedrock fractures, channel transmission loss, and existing pool water can materially change actual conditions.
NOAA Atlas 14 values are point-frequency context, not direct proof of runoff or pool condition.

zerog.name: ZeroG
zerog.storage_basis: Zero G 1-meter depression inventory: 114 depressions totaling 1,485.0 m3 (52,442 ft3)
zerog.calibration: measured Zero G depression storage + federal basin data

black-hole-white-canyon.calibration: technical-length normalized; morphology-adjusted; field calibration needed
